agent_notes: Greenwich is strongest for museums, heritage walks and markets; Books & Coffee is the binding constraint under PAINTS v6.
Several operational details, especially group walk-in tolerance and Google review floors, require host confirmation before publishing.
NOW Gallery is excluded because live Greenwich Peninsula sources say it is permanently closed.

venues.opening_hours: Official/Visit Greenwich/Vintners Yard list lunch and dinner seven days; Visit Greenwich hours show Mon/Sun 12:00-22:00, Tue-Sat 12:00-23:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Restaurant accepts bookings; walk-in acceptance for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable if seated in wine bar/dining room, but group walk-in must be called ahead.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic wine vaults and restaurant format; exact 8-12 capacity UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.why_fits_paints: A long-running Greenwich wine merchant setting gives immediate Show & Tell material without feeling like a generic bar. The restored-cellar character and large wine list suit object-led conversation around bottles, labels and local history. It is central enough to be practical but still has a tucked-away Vintners Yard texture.
venues.anecdote_story: Davy's is tied to a long London wine-merchant lineage and Vintners Yard frames the venue as part of Greenwich's older trading fabric.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine bar, modern British food, Davy's merchant wines and Friday tasting culture at the adjacent wine shop.
venues.safety_notes: Central Greenwich with strong evening footfall and quick access to Greenwich station/DLR; use lit high-street route after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Potentially busy at dinner; walk-in for 8-12 is the main unresolved gate.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Tue-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-21:00, Mon closed; food Tue-Sat 12:00-21:00, Sun 12:00-19:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings available; walk-in acceptance for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Two floors and event capacity listed by Visit Greenwich make an 8-12 group plausible with heads-up.
venues.capacity_notes: Visit Greenwich notes events up to 60; normal service seating for PAINTS group still host-confirm.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Georgian corner pub with a leafy garden gives a quieter Greenwich counterweight to riverside tourist traffic. The food-and-wine positioning is more considered than a standard pub. It works as a bottle-and-object conversation venue when Davy's is too central or booked.
venues.anecdote_story: The building is presented locally as a handsome Georgian tavern and the current kitchen is led by chef Guy Awford.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fine wines, local beers, cocktails and seasonal modern British food.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Greenwich side street; route from Greenwich station is short but use well-lit Royal Hill/Greenwich High Road after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed Mondays; garden can be weather-sensitive.

venues.opening_hours: All Things Greenwich lists Mon-Thu 17:00-23:30, Fri 17:00-00:00, Sat 13:00-00:00, Sun 13:00-23:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine-bar format; walk-in acceptance for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable only if an early table area can be held; host must call.
venues.capacity_notes: Small French wine bar; 8-12 may need advance heads-up.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is one of the clearest category fits in the district: a non-chain Blackheath wine bar with more than 100 wines and a French-led list. It gives a gentler village alternative to Greenwich town centre. The intimate scale suits show-and-tell prompts around regional wine, labels and cheese/charcuterie.
venues.anecdote_story: Formerly Le Bouchon, it has been written up as an independently owned Blackheath wine bar in a high street otherwise vulnerable to chains.
venues.serve_or_on_view: French and international wines by glass, carafe and bottle; cheese and charcuterie boards.
venues.safety_notes: Blackheath Village has strong station proximity; use Tranquil Vale directly to Blackheath station after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Small room and weekend crowding; group walk-in unverified.

venues.opening_hours: Visit Greenwich lists Tue-Wed 10:00-18:30, Thu-Sat 10:00-20:00, Sun 10:00-18:30, Mon closed. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Retail wine shop; tasting/group drink-in availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Demoted because drink-in/group format is not clearly verified online.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent shop rather than bar; needs host arrangement.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday-Friday 17:30-19:30 if shop can host tastings.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is independent, local and wine-led, with stronger taste value than a generic pub backup. It could work as a guided bottle-led meet if the shop confirms tasting permission. Without that confirmation it is operationally fragile, hence Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: Blackheath village wine retail gives a local merchant story and supports the area's independent high-street identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine retail and likely advice-led bottle browsing; group tasting not confirmed.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Blackheath station on active village streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 2 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: May not be a sit-down or walk-in drinks venue; host must confirm before use.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Mon-Thu noon-midnight, Fri-Sat noon-01:00, Sun noon-23:00; Visit Greenwich lists daily 12:00-23:00 and food 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format suggests walk-ins; acceptance for 8-12 at live-music times UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for sociable groups, but avoid live-band crush unless PAINTS wants a louder session.
venues.capacity_notes: Live-music local pub with food; table configuration must be checked.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday-Thursday 18:00-20:00, or Sunday before music if programmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Pelton has the real-local Greenwich energy PAINTS likes: live music, regulars and non-polished charm. It is useful for Show & Tell themes around records, band posters or local performance. It counterbalances the more heritage-heavy core of the borough.
venues.anecdote_story: Musicians' Union lists it as a Fair Play music venue, and local listings repeatedly flag its live-band reputation.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, pub drinks, pizza/street food, live music nights.
venues.safety_notes: Residential East Greenwich; attendees should leave in pairs or use Maze Hill/Cutty Sark routes on lit roads.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 8 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Noise may undermine conversation on live-music nights; check event calendar.

venues.opening_hours: Visit Greenwich lists Tue-Fri 16:00-23:00, Sat-Sun 12:00-23:00, Mon closed. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub/restaurant format; group walk-in acceptance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely better with a heads-up call; demoted for unverified group seating.
venues.capacity_notes: Small character pub on Royal Hill; exact table grouping unverified.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has more character and local identity than most central Greenwich pubs. Royal Hill gives a village-like conversation setting away from the tourist river line. It is a strong backup rather than a first pick because the wine/craft specificity is lighter than Davy's or Le Bar A Vin.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Hill's old Greenwich village setting supplies the story; specific owner backstory UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and food; exact wine/craft emphasis UNVERIFIED.
venues.safety_notes: Short, well-used walk from Greenwich station; use Royal Hill and Greenwich High Road after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 1 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed Mondays; less category-specific than a true wine bar.

venues.opening_hours: Visit Greenwich lists Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-22:30; food Mon-Sat 12:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-21:00. Bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format; group walk-in acceptance for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally useful Royal Hill backup, but category fit is lighter.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional pub; exact group seating unverified.
venues.why_fits_paints: A reliable Royal Hill pub gives an easy fallback for a Greenwich drinks event. It has local charm and proximity to stronger primary venues. It is Tier B because it is less distinctive and more obvious than the wine-led choices.
venues.anecdote_story: Specific backstory not found in live sources; Royal Hill location is the main contextual story.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and food.
venues.safety_notes: Short route to Greenwich station via well-used streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 1 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Less PAINTS-coded; use only when better wine-led options are unavailable.

venues.opening_hours: Official market: daily 10:00-17:30 including bank holidays, closed Christmas Day and first six Mondays of year.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if meeting outside main aisles; split browsing then regroup.
venues.capacity_notes: Busy historic covered market; avoid narrow central pinch points.
venues.best_time_slot: Friday 11:00-13:30 for crafts plus food, or weekday 14:00-16:00 for quieter browsing.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is the borough's best all-category anchor for maker-led browsing, street food and visible craft. The covered setting helps weather resilience. Its history inside Maritime Greenwich gives the group a shared story without needing a formal tour.
venues.anecdote_story: The market trades within the Maritime Greenwich World Heritage setting and mixes arts, crafts, antiques and food on different days.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Arts and crafts, antiques and collectables, food and drink stalls.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall town centre; clear transit via Cutty Sark DLR and Greenwich station.
venues.backup_venue: Cutty Sark Street Food Market / Greenwich riverfront food fallback
venues.backup_walk_time: About 4 min walk by Google Maps estimate; alternative food market status UNVERIFIED - host must confirm
venues.risks_watchouts: Tourist-heavy and obvious; apply pacing to keep it from becoming generic.

venues.opening_hours: London Farmers' Markets and Visit Greenwich list every Sunday 10:00-14:00; LFM says rain or shine and usually only closes between Christmas and New Year.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access farmers market; meeting point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for slow producer-led browsing; group should meet at station edge.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor station-car-park market; weather plan needed.
venues.why_fits_paints: It has the producer focus PAINTS wants and a clear village feel. Blackheath gives Greenwich borough geographic balance away from the maritime core. The market pairs naturally with a safety-listed Blackheath walk or wine-bar finish.
venues.anecdote_story: LFM notes weekly trading rain or shine; Royal Greenwich local history adds Blackheath's pilgrimage and Peasants' Revolt backstory nearby.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, prepared food and rotating local stallholders.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; Blackheath is on PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist if paired with a walk.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by transit via rail/DLR or bus; within market backup rule
venues.risks_watchouts: Outdoor and only Sunday; stall mix varies weekly.

venues.opening_hours: Royal Greenwich: second and fourth Saturday every month except December, 10:00-15:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point at Woolwich Works Visitors' Book Cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good: broad public square, stalls and nearby cultural fallback.
venues.capacity_notes: Over 40 stalls listed by council; open-air.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Greenwich borough's best Woolwich counterweight: producer stalls, makers, food and the Royal Arsenal heritage setting. The square gives enough space for matched strangers to circulate without bottlenecking. Live music/entertainment noted by the council adds warmth.
venues.anecdote_story: Artillery Square sits inside the former Royal Arsenal, turning a market day into a light industrial-heritage event.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, meat, cheese, jams, chutneys, local makers and street food.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Woolwich Arsenal precinct, close to Elizabeth line and DLR; use main lit routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Only twice monthly and not in December; weather-sensitive.

venues.opening_hours: Royal Greenwich: second and fourth Saturday every month except December, 10:00-15:00; Visit Greenwich separately lists fourth Sunday 10:00-14:00, so schedule needs host confirmation.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for daytime outdoor browsing; stall density variable.
venues.capacity_notes: Open-air market; smaller than Greenwich/Royal Arsenal.
venues.why_fits_paints: Eltham adds a valuable outer-Greenwich counterweight and a less-obvious local feel. The maker/food mix fits PAINTS if the market is active that weekend. It is Tier B because public sources conflict on cadence.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Greenwich local history notes Eltham's royal associations and Chaucer connection, useful as a nearby prompt.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Candles, clothes, food and drink; first market of the month includes community stalls and free arts/crafts per council.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street location; confirm meeting point near main footfall.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-day market backup within 15 min confirmed; closest thematic alternative by transit is Severndroog Castle Producers Market if dates align
venues.risks_watchouts: Conflicting schedule sources; must verify date before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Royal Greenwich lists Mon-Wed 09:00-16:00, Thu 09:00-13:00, Fri-Sat 09:00-16:30; bank holiday times may vary.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally easy but less curated; use as Woolwich backup only.
venues.capacity_notes: Open-air general market dating back to the 1600s per council.
venues.why_fits_paints: Its age and Woolwich everyday texture are interesting, but the offer is more general-goods than curated makers. It works as a backup when Royal Arsenal Producers Market is not running. Tier B because PAINTS atmosphere is uneven.
venues.anecdote_story: Council source describes the market as dating back to the 1600s.
venues.serve_or_on_view: General goods, fresh food, household products and hot food.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Woolwich town centre; use main station approaches.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate when Royal Arsenal is operating
venues.risks_watchouts: Less maker-led, more bargain/general market; only use with careful host framing.

venues.opening_hours: Royal Greenwich directory notes Kidbrooke Village Sunday Market; Instagram says new 2026 fourth Sunday each month. Exact hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially suitable if the market is active; host must verify stall count and atmosphere.
venues.capacity_notes: Village square market; scale unknown.
venues.best_time_slot: Fourth Sunday late morning, only after confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: Kidbrooke is useful for geographic spread and could surface small local businesses. The market is less established in live sources, so it is an operational backup rather than a launch hero. It should only run after a host scout visit.
venues.anecdote_story: The listing frames it around small businesses, community stalls and fresh bread/cheese/coffee.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh bread, cheese, coffee, gifts, small businesses and community stalls per council listing.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; use Kidbrooke station and main Pegler Square routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10-15 min by bus/rail depending on service; within market backup transit rule
venues.risks_watchouts: New/limited market; official hours are not fully visible in indexed sources.

venues.venue_name: Greenwich Park Rose Garden to Observatory Walk
venues.neighbourhood_station: Greenwich Park / Maze Hill or Cutty Sark DLR
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks: pedestrian gates 06:00-21:30 in current source; Friends of Greenwich Park lists June/July 06:00-21:00. Use daytime/early evening only.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; Ignatius Sancho Cafe meeting point walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 in daylight with clear start/end and no secluded route.
venues.capacity_notes: Open park route; meeting point capacity at cafe unverified.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:30-12:30, or weekday 16:00-18:00 in summer.
venues.why_fits_paints: Greenwich Park is on the PAINTS Premium Launch Parks list and has dense visual prompts without needing a long walk. Stops: Rose Garden, Flower Garden, Ranger's House exterior, Royal Observatory viewpoint. The route is safe, legible and story-rich.
venues.anecdote_story: The Flower Garden was laid out as a formal horticultural showpiece, while the new cafe references Ignatius Sancho, the Georgian abolitionist, writer and composer associated with Greenwich.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, rose garden, formal flower beds, Ranger's House exterior and Observatory skyline.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Premium Launch Park confirmed. Avoid after dark and keep to main paths.
venues.backup_walk_time: Cafe at/near start; Queen's House about 5 min from lower park gates
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and park closing time; cafe walk-in/group tolerance unverified.

venues.venue_name: Greenwich Park One Tree Hill and Flower Garden Walk
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks: pedestrian gates 06:00-21:30; Friends list June/July 06:00-21:00. Use daylight only.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park; meeting cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for an 8-12 walking circle; route should stay on main paths.
venues.capacity_notes: Open park route.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the quieter companion route to the Observatory-heavy walk. Stops: Blackheath Gate, Flower Garden, Queen's Orchard area, One Tree Hill viewpoint. It preserves the Premium-park safety gate while avoiding the most tourist-dense river approach.
venues.anecdote_story: Friends of Greenwich Park links the park's formal avenues to the 1660s and the Flower Garden to Victorian-style planting.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Formal planting, old chestnut avenues, skyline viewpoints and park architecture.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Premium Launch Park confirmed. Avoid secluded edges and after-dark finish.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of Blackheath Gate area; exact venue walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup cafe choice needs host scout; do not use park in poor light.

venues.opening_hours: Open common; use daytime only. Lewisham Council notes public-benefit status and temporary toilets 11:00-20:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access common; Le Bar A Vin weather backup walk-in for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daylight if staying near village/heath edges.
venues.capacity_notes: Open common; no enclosed capacity issues.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 11:00-13:00 paired with farmers' market, or late afternoon before wine bar opening.
venues.why_fits_paints: Blackheath is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and gives huge story value in a compact route. Stops: Blackheath station/village, heath edge, All Saints church view, Tranquil Vale independent shopfronts. It is less scenic-garden polished than Greenwich Park but more socially textured.
venues.anecdote_story: Royal Greenwich history notes Blackheath as a gathering place for pilgrims, armies and Wat Tyler's Peasants' Revolt crowd in 1381.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Open heath, village architecture, church views and independent shopfronts.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist confirmed. Daytime only; keep group near village edge and station routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 3-5 min from village/heath edge
venues.risks_watchouts: Large open space can feel exposed in bad weather or darkness; avoid night walks.

venues.venue_name: Woolwich Royal Arsenal Heritage Precinct Walk
venues.opening_hours: Public precinct open access; Woolwich Works cafe/bar listed daily by Visit Greenwich. Exact estate access/closures UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access precinct; Woolwich Works Visitors' Book Cafe walk-in for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good in daytime/early evening around main Royal Arsenal streets and squares.
venues.capacity_notes: Open public realm; indoor fallback capacity unverified.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 11:00-13:00, ideally paired with Royal Arsenal Producers Market.
venues.why_fits_paints: Royal Arsenal precinct is explicitly permitted in PAINTS v6 under Riverside/Industrial Heritage. Stops: Woolwich station arrival, Artillery Square, Woolwich Works exterior, Thames-side Arsenal frontage. It gives a strong industrial-design story outside Greenwich's expected maritime core.
venues.anecdote_story: The former Royal Arsenal's military-industrial past now sits beside a contemporary cultural hub and producer market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Industrial heritage buildings, public squares, river frontage and Woolwich Works cultural architecture.
venues.safety_notes: Permitted PAINTS non-park route. Use daytime/early evening and stay in the active Royal Arsenal precinct.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of Artillery Square/start
venues.risks_watchouts: Check for private events, closures and construction diversions.

venues.opening_hours: RMG: open daily 10:00-17:00, last entry 16:15, closed 24-26 Dec; free entry but ticket recommended/required.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free entry but ticket booking recommended; timed/group availability for event date UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a slow looking group if tickets are secured.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum/gallery circulation suits 8-12; avoid peak school/holiday times.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is the district's most PAINTS-perfect museum/gallery venue: painting, architecture and maritime identity in one compact place. Current 2026 contemporary additions give fresh material beyond the permanent collection. The Inigo Jones building makes casual photos striking even in grey weather.
venues.anecdote_story: The Queen's House is Inigo Jones's pioneering classical building and now holds major maritime art, including the Armada Portrait context and 2026 contemporary works.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Internationally renowned art, architecture, Armada Portrait context and 2026 contemporary works including Tam Joseph's Migrants display.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall museum quarter; easy daylight routes to Cutty Sark DLR and Greenwich station.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 3 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Free ticketing can still cap entry; confirm availability.

venues.venue_name: National Maritime Museum - Windrush Food Culture
venues.opening_hours: RMG: National Maritime Museum open daily 10:00-17:00; Windrush Food Culture daily until 30 July 2026, free entry.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum entry with booking recommended; exhibition/date availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong, especially with food-memory discussion prompts.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; exhibition gallery capacity/date must be confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: This exhibition gives a social-history lens that can turn strangers into storytellers. It fits PAINTS by connecting objects, food culture and migration memory. The museum is operationally robust and has multiple nearby backups.
venues.anecdote_story: The exhibition explores how Windrush food culture moved from the margins to the mainstream in Britain.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Windrush food culture exhibition and wider maritime/social-history displays.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall museum campus with clear station routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 3 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition ends 30 July 2026; do not use after that date without replacement.

venues.venue_name: National Maritime Museum - Kõmij Mour Ijin / Our Life is Here
venues.opening_hours: RMG: open daily 10:00-17:00; exhibition open daily until 4 October 2026, free.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum entry with booking recommended; group/timed availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a reflective group; host should set tone carefully.
venues.capacity_notes: Large museum; gallery crowding must be checked.
venues.why_fits_paints: The exhibition's climate, memory and nuclear-testing subject matter gives real story depth. It is current, free and conversation-worthy. It is less conventional than maritime greatest-hits displays, which helps the PAINTS filter.
venues.anecdote_story: RMG frames the show around reflections on nuclear testing and climate change in the Marshall Islands.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary art exhibition and related museum collections.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall museum campus; use Cutty Sark/Greenwich routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 3 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Sensitive subject matter; host should avoid making it too heavy for first-time matched strangers.

venues.venue_name: The Fan Museum - Monarchy: An Exhibition of Royal Fans
venues.opening_hours: Official site: Tue-Sat 11:00-17:00, final admissions 16:30; current exhibition 9 May-26 September 2026.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official site says book in advance online or buy in person on the day; group availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent object-led conversation but small museum; host must confirm group entry.
venues.capacity_notes: Small specialist museum; cap group or stagger if required.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the kind of specific, overlooked cultural object PAINTS should love. Fans are intimate design objects with social codes, materials and royal stories. It gives a quieter, more tactile alternative to the large RMG campus.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum is devoted to fan-making and display; 2026 show focuses on royal fans and monarchy.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Royal fans, fan-making craft and permanent fan collections.
venues.safety_notes: Crooms Hill is close to Greenwich town centre; use daytime/early evening only.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 7 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed Sunday/Monday; small-site capacity and day tickets must be checked.

venues.venue_name: Old Royal Naval College - Painted Hall
venues.opening_hours: Official ORNC: Painted Hall, Chapel and Visitor Centre daily 10:00-17:00, last entry 16:30; grounds 08:00-23:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed; walk-up/timed availability for 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong if tickets secured; grand interior supports slow-looking prompts.
venues.capacity_notes: Large hall but timed entry/tour flow may affect groups.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Painted Hall is obvious, but it earns the penalty back through sheer visual quality and story density. It supports a 2-hour art-history conversation without needing too much walking. It is a reliable bad-weather museum pick.
venues.anecdote_story: The ORNC story runs from Greenwich Palace and naval hospital history into the Baroque Painted Hall; official tours run from the Visitor Centre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Painted Hall, Chapel, Visitor Centre, tours and the Maritime Greenwich ensemble.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall heritage campus, close to Cutty Sark DLR and river bus.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Tourist and school-group crowding; ticket availability must be checked.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Greenwich Park / Cutty Sark DLR or Maze Hill
venues.opening_hours: RMG: standard 10:00-17:00, summer June-August until 19:00, September until 18:00; check closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed; timed/group availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good but tourist-heavy; better as backup or science/story variant.
venues.capacity_notes: Timed/ticketed attraction; group movement can be constrained at peak times.
venues.why_fits_paints: The time/longitude story is excellent but less directly painting/craft-led than Queen's House or Fan Museum. It can work for a science-and-object conversation variant. Tier B due to obviousness, ticketing and category fit.
venues.anecdote_story: The Observatory's 350-year navigation/timekeeping legacy is the primary narrative hook.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Astronomy, timekeeping, navigation objects, Prime Meridian and planetarium-related material.
venues.safety_notes: Inside Greenwich Park, a PAINTS Premium Launch Park; avoid late exits through dark park routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10 min downhill walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a pure art/gallery pick; tourist density and timed ticketing.

venues.opening_hours: University of Greenwich Galleries lists Drawing Thinking 12 Jun-31 Jul 2026; Instagram says Stephen Lawrence Gallery Tue-Fri 11:00-17:00, Sat 11:00-16:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public university gallery; walk-in and group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if gallery confirms no private teaching/event conflict.
venues.capacity_notes: Small-to-medium public gallery; host must verify.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the best contemporary-art counterweight to the heritage cluster. The current Drawing Thinking show is conceptually rich and current to July 2026. It gives PAINTS a less tourist-obvious Greenwich option.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery was founded in 2000 after the Macpherson report and aims to promote diversity in visual culture.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Drawing Thinking, an interdisciplinary exhibition on drawing as conceptualisation, experimentation and discovery.
venues.safety_notes: University/campus edge in central Greenwich; use Stockwell Street and Cutty Sark/Greenwich routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 6 min walk by Google Maps estimate
venues.risks_watchouts: Exhibition ends 31 July 2026; university closures/private events must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: Royal Greenwich Heritage Trust page: weekdays 09:30-15:30, Saturdays 09:30-15:00, Sundays closed; Christmas/Easter/occasional closures flagged.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free entry listed; group walk-in and current room access UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible but operationally fragile because house openings and exhibitions can vary.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic rooms; exact group circulation unverified.
venues.why_fits_paints: Charlton House adds borough balance and a historic-house texture missing from central Greenwich. The 100 exhibitions give a civic-memory angle. It is Tier B because exhibition strength and opening reliability are weaker than the main museum cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: The exhibitions mark 100 years of public ownership of Charlton House.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Charlton House 100 displays in The Study and The White Room; historic Jacobean house context.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; use main Charlton Road approach and confirm transport plan.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min; closest cultural alternative Woolwich Works is about 15-20 min by bus, so primary remains Tier B
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight same-category backup; occasional closures; limited current press.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Live Greenwich Peninsula and Tripadvisor sources report the gallery permanently closed; not operational for PAINTS.
Strong community-pub story, but Instagram/search and local press indicate closure/sale uncertainty; fails operational gate.
Good Argentine wine-list angle, but official site posts a May 2026 temporary closure notice following an incident; fails current operational gate.
Beautiful independent bookshop with strong history, but no verified on-site cafe or literal independent coffee partner next door; fails PAINTS Books & Coffee requirement.
Excellent bookshop-cafe signal, but current branch is in Lee/Lewisham rather than Greenwich district and official site flags a July 14-27 closure; not suitable for this Greenwich file.
Book offer is operationally clear but chain ownership conflicts with PAINTS independent/no-chain filter; cafe/coffee pairing not strong enough to override.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: The O2 / Icon Outlet food-and-drink venues
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Inside a mall/arena destination and dominated by chain/templated venues; fails PAINTS aesthetic filter.
Not in Greenwich district and PAINTS safety list marks Mudchute paused/daytime family only; not sourceable for Creative Walks.
